qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Code execution via callable arguments in ImageMath.unsafe_eval()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a FITS GZIP decompression bomb (CWE-400/CWE-770) in Pillow's FITS decoder where unbounded GZIP data causes OOM. This finding concerns ImageMath.unsafe_eval() allowing callable kwargs to bypass eval() security filters — a different Pillow component (ImageMath vs. FITS), different root cause (eval filter bypass vs. missing decompression size limit), and different vulnerability class (code injection vs. resource consumption).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a GZIP decompression bomb in Pillow's FITS image decoder (CWE-400/CWE-770), where unbounded decompressed data causes OOM/DoS. The qualifying finding 'imagemath_rce' is a code injection via ImageMath.unsafe_eval() callable kwargs — a different Pillow component with a different root cause (eval filter bypass) and different vulnerability class (code injection) than the advisory's FITS GZIP decompression bomb. It does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code path (FITS decoder GZIP decompression without size limits).
evidence: Advisory root cause: Pillow FITS decoder reads unlimited GZIP-compressed data, causing unbounded memory consumption (src/PIL/FitsImagePlugin.py, PR #9521, commit 3cb854e8).
Finding 'imagemath_rce' targets src/PIL/ImageMath.py:267-310 — ImageMath component, not the FITS decoder; root cause is eval() accepting callable kwargs, not missing GZIP decompression size limits.
Advisory CWE-400/CWE-770 (resource consumption) does not match the finding's vulnerability class (code injection).
